cases.description: Population standard deviation on the SAME dataset {2,4,4,4,5,5,7,9} used for STDEV/STDEV.S - divides by n instead of n-1, giving a smaller, exact result (2) vs the sample stdev's 2.138089935299395
Same two-point data as the sample-stdev cases (10,20) but population formula divides by n=2 instead of n-1=1, giving a much smaller result
cases.expected_note: Population variance=((10-15)^2+(20-15)^2)/2=25; sqrt(25)=5, vs sample stdev's 7.0710678118654755 on identical data

cases.description: Unlike STDEV.S/STDEV, a single value does NOT error for the population formula, since it divides by n (=1), not n-1
cases.expected_note: Population variance of one point = 0/1 = 0, a valid result - genuine divergence from STDEV.S's #DIV/0! on the same single-cell input
